pattern: The skip applies to the single continuous zigzag walk as a whole, not row by row, so simulating the walk while alternating a keep/drop flag across every row boundary reproduces the kept sequence directly.
hints: Concatenate the zigzag visiting order (left-to-right on even rows, right-to-left on odd rows) into one long sequence, ignoring the skip for a moment.

The skip alternates over that whole concatenated sequence, not separately per row -- so whether a given cell is kept depends on how many cells have been visited so far in total, carried across row boundaries.
Walk the grid row by row in zigzag order with a single running counter; keep a cell exactly when that counter is even, then advance the counter after every visited cell regardless of row.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two by two grid
statement example, three by three grid
odd number of columns keeps the skip alternation carrying across rows correctly
single-column-per-row pattern still alternates direction each row

stress.description: large random grid near the maximum size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(2, min(size, 50))
    n = max(2, min(size, 50))
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 2500) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
